description_long: This object consists of several irregularly shaped, dark rocks with a rugged, volcanic appearance. The surfaces are predominantly black with scattered, glowing orange-red patches that suggest embedded molten material or recent exposure to intense heat. The rocks vary in size, with the largest piece being elongated and curved, while the smaller fragments are more rounded or angular. The texture appears coarse and pitted, with visible crevices and rough edges. The coloration and form evoke the look of cooled lava or basalt, making the rocks visually striking and distinct. These rocks could serve as natural barriers or decorative elements in an urban landscape.
description: A group of dark, rugged volcanic rocks with glowing orange-red patches and a coarse, pitted texture.
description_view_0: Two large, curved rocks dominate the left side, with several smaller fragments scattered to the right; glowing orange-red patches are visible on the surfaces.
description_view_1: The rocks are seen from above, revealing their irregular shapes and the distribution of the glowing patches across the surfaces.
description_view_2: The largest rocks are positioned on the right, showing their curved profiles and rough, pitted surfaces, with smaller pieces spread out to the left.
description_view_3: The largest rock is prominent on the right, displaying its curved face and vivid orange-red highlights, while smaller rocks are visible to the left.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped plaza as a decorative or barrier element
